summary: Terrapin debut, fiery Help>Slip>Franklin's, tight jams all night
blurb: The concert opens with the first live rendition of "Terrapin Station" and a crisp "Estimated Prophet." The second set erupts into an iconic Help On The Way → Slipknot → Franklin's Tower sequence plus a monster Eyes Of The World jam. Jerry’s guitar soars, Phil’s bass punches, and Keith’s keyboards drive the energy.
review:
February 26, 1977 at Swing Auditorium captured one of the Grateful Dead’s most celebrated shows of the year. Not only did the band unveil "Terrapin Station" in its debut performance—a lower‑key, soaring opener that set a high‑energy tone—but they also rolled out the brand‑new "Estimated Prophet" with electric urgency. The first set flowed smoothly through classics like "Sugaree" and the seamless Playing > Wheel > Playing transition, showcasing a band locked in tight groove.

The second set is where legends are forged: a smoking Help On The Way → Slipknot → Franklin's Tower run that fans still cite as “smoking” and “perfectly lilted,” followed by an expansive Eyes Of The World jam that highlights Phil’s rare bass solo. The show closes with an aggressive Around & Around and the ever‑crowd‑pleasing encore "U.S. Blues." Across all available recordings, the Betty Board soundboard capture stands out for its crystal‑clear mix, preserving every nuance of Jerry’s blazing guitar tones, Keith’s slamming piano/Polymoog textures, and the twin‑drum engine of Mickey and Billy.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All eleven recordings are overwhelmingly positive; multiple reviewers call the performance "legendary," "blazing," and note a seamless, high‑energy flow throughout both sets. Even the single mixed review still praises key moments (Eyes Of The World, Slipknot) and only flags track breaks, not musical flaws. No consistent negative commentary about playing was found, just occasional recording glitches.

best_recording.reason: Betty Board soundboard capture praised as crystal‑clear, balanced and free of hiss or dropouts; it showcases the full detail of the performance.

key_highlights: Debut of "Terrapin Station" and first live showing of "Estimated Prophet"
"Help On The Way → Slipknot → Franklin's Tower" sequence described as smoking and perfect
Monster Eyes Of The World jam with a rare Phil bass solo
Tight Playing > Wheel > Playing transition and aggressive Around & Around

band_performance.Jerry: His guitar tone is described as blazing, well‑formed and crystal‑clear; standout solos on Slipknot and the Help sequence, plus a soaring lead during Terrapin Station.
band_performance.Phil: Flat‑picked Alembic bass delivers punchy foundation; rare, praised solo in Eyes Of The World earns a "Phil bonus."
band_performance.Bob: Vocals and rhythm work are strong, with highlighted moments on Sugaree and energetic crowd interaction ("Hey Bob").
band_performance.Keith: Piano is slamming and beautifully phrased; adds Polymoog texture on Estimated Prophet and occasional Moog flourishes that enrich the set.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums tight, crisp and driving the groove throughout both sets.
band_performance.Billy: Provides solid, propulsive back‑beat complementing Mickey’s rhythms, keeping the high‑energy flow.
